title: crataegus monogyna subsp. azarella
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. azarella is a subspecies of hawthorn, a type of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae), native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The hawthorn has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various cardiovascular conditions, including high blood pressure, heart failure, and angina, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's berries, flowers, and leaves are rich in vitamins, minerals, and flavonoids, which have been shown to have numerous health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving blood lipid profiles, and lowering blood pressure. Hawthorn is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
